next_step_text: 6/29 - Manscaped issued a new RFP for Procurement Only vs P2P - Responses due 7/16 and Decision due 8/16 ﻿6/9 - Shared updated pricing to reflect limited scope. Manscaped will respond with next steps by 6/18 ﻿6/2 - Vatricia (POC) confirmed after reviewing additional materials shared, they do not need to schedule a follow up demo. They are currently making a decision. I mentioned we are happy to ge

our_open_deliverables.text: Confirm via email whether supplier management module stores quality/specification documents
Schedule the follow-up eProcurement and Insights Studio demo with Vatricia

buyer_dependent_items.text: Respond with next steps by 18 June re: updated Procurement-Only pricing shared 9 June
Issue the formal Procurement-Only RFP responses decision by 16 August
Share procurement requirements document (gated on signed NDA)

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the incumbent being displaced on renewal and remains the primary current threat, alongside the real 'Do Nothing / stay on Coupa' path the buyer signalled on 2 Jun. Our wedge is concrete and recency-confirmed: Coupa's poor UI, PO-not-tied-to-invoice, and clunky NetSuite integration (Becca West, 1 Apr). LinkSquares is a CLM integration requirement, not a procurement rival, and is dormant as a threat. No other procurement vendor is named in any source.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: we're on Coupa... the UI is probably the main struggle... the PO isn't tied to the invoice... the connection with Netsuite is clunky at best
competitors.how_we_win: Lean on AI-native, fully integrated single-platform with clean NetSuite + LinkSquares + Teams integration and a midsize-appropriate, simple UI; quantify tail-spend savings Coupa is not delivering.

competitors.name: Do Nothing
competitors.quote: Scope narrowed 29 June from P2P to Procurement-Only (Next Step 29 June). Buyer committed 18 June reply per 9 June Next Step but was 11 days late, re-engaging 29 June with new RFP. May signal budget constraint or internal re-prioritization.
competitors.source: Next Step 29 June 2026; Next Step 9 June 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Build urgency by tying procurement centralization and tail-spend control to immediate cost avoidance and compliance risk mitigation. Co-author ROI model with Vatricia and Becca showing payback within 6 months. Secure Economic Buyer meeting to validate budget and timeline before 16 July RFP deadline. If buyer defers, Coupa renewal will lock them in for another cycle.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / stay on Coupa
competitors.quote: do we wanna stay with our vendor? Do we wanna maybe move, and kind of starting that process
competitors.source: Avoma intro call 1 Apr 2026 (Becca West); SF Next Step 2 Jun 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Make the Coupa renewal a forcing event; sharpen the limited-scope price and quantify the cost of staying fragmented so inertia is the expensive option.

competitors.name: LinkSquares
competitors.quote: we do use Linksquares... do you guys integrate with that?
competitors.how_we_win: Integrate cleanly with LinkSquares as required; optionally position Zycus iContract as a future consolidation play without forcing a CLM rip-and-replace now.

champion_strength.source: Avoma intro call 1 Apr 2026; SF Next Step 13 May 2026
champion_strength.summary: Anita Newsom is a genuine but currently passive champion (referral source, prior Zycus customer at Ventura Foods) who has been off calls since 1 Apr 2026; day-to-day ownership sits with neutral evaluator Vatricia McClurg. Net strength is developing/at-risk.

sources: SF buyer contact roles (Lisa Miller, Becca West)
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has surfaced on any call. All three conversations were mid-level: Anita Newsom (Senior Director of Procurement, the referral source), Becca West (Director of Financial & Accounting Systems), Lisa Miller (Purchasing Manager) and Vatricia McClurg (procurement project owner, 'POC'). At a $130k ACV a director-level group can often approve, but no budget owner or signatory has been named or engaged, and the approval chain is undocumented. Account-level contacts who could be the EB (Eric Lam, SVP Finance & Accounting) have not appeared on a call for THIS opportunity. The move is for the deal owner to ask Vatricia directly who signs and what the approval path is, and to seek an introduction to the finance budget owner before the 16 Aug decision.

recommended_moves: Submit a tight Procurement-Only RFP response leading with the NetSuite, LinkSquares and Teams integration wedge.
Ask Vatricia who approves the decision and request an introduction to the finance budget owner.
Re-engage Anita Newsom to coach the RFP response and reinforce her Ventura Foods reference internally.
